title: Getting Started & Getting Help Overview
subtitle: Listen to the lesson explained in English

transcript.en: Hey! 👋 Unit 3. The survival kit — how to get help when your Maltese runs out.
Three things: **phrases to use when you're stuck**, **nationalities**, and a neat grammar point about **asking politely**. Three minutes. Let's go!
💼 PART 1 — ASKING FOR HELP 💼
Everyone starts at 'I don't speak Maltese'. Say it proudly:
Or the better opener — I speak a little:
Even better — show you're trying:
That phrase means 'I am learning Maltese'. Say this and you will get smiles every time. 😊
When you lose the thread — I don't understand:
Sorry, I didn't understand:
Can you repeat that?
Slowly, please:
Can you write it down?
Can you help me?
Note the spelling of that last phrase — it ends in a double n. That's the correct form, always. 🔑
How do you say that in Maltese?
What does it mean?
Do you speak English?
And a useful anchor — I work here, I belong:
I'm a foreigner — remember both forms, masculine and feminine:
These phrases are your safety net. Ten phrases and you can navigate almost anything in Malta. 🛟
The mini dialogue in the unit shows how these stack up. Someone asks if you speak Maltese:

transcript.mt: Skużani — ma nitkellimx Malti tajjeb. Titkellem bl-Ingliż?

transcript.en: Sorry, I don't speak Maltese well. Do you speak English? And the reply:
Yes, a little. How can I help you? Always a kind reply in Malta. ☕
🌍 PART 2 — NATIONALITIES 🌍
Nationalities in Maltese always have two forms — one for a man, one for a woman. Get both.
Maltese — the two forms:
English:
Italian:
French:
German:
Spanish:
American:
And the universal fallback — foreigner:
Pattern: most masculine forms end in a consonant or 'i'; the feminine adds **-a**. Easy once you see it. 🎯
📖 PART 3 — GRAMMAR: BEING POLITE 📖
Maltese politeness works by **stacking**. You just pile small polite words on top of each other. 😄
Plain thanks:
Warmer — thanks a lot:
Full polite request — excuse me, can you help me?
Yes please:
No thanks:
The more you stack, the warmer it sounds. There's no upper limit on Maltese politeness! 🌟
Now the most useful request structure in the whole language — **Tista'…?**
This word is simply 'you can'. Put any verb after it for a polite ask. Repeat?
Help me?
Write it down?
Same structure every time. Gloss it as **'Can you…?'** — never 'Could you…?' in Maltese. ✅
The full set of 'can' covers I, you, and he — three little words, infinitely useful.
One last tip: nearly everyone in Malta speaks great English — but leading with even one phrase of Maltese earns enormous goodwill. Try:
Then switch to English guilt-free. You've already won! 😄
✅ DONE!
Help phrases. Nationalities. Politeness through stacking. The Tista' structure. That's Unit 3.
The exercises will drill these in. Every phrase you learn here makes life in Malta easier. Keep going! 💪
Saħħa! 🇲🇹
